pages.text: Tayo can’t wait to meet his baby sister. Hurry up Mama! Let her out of your tummy.

blocks.text: Tayo can’t wait to meet his baby sister. Hurry up Mama! Let her out of your tummy.

pages.text: But Tinu isn’t what Tayo expects. She looks funny. She smells horrible! And even worse…

blocks.text: But Tinu isn’t what Tayo expects. She looks funny.
She smells horrible!
And even worse…

pages.text: Neh! Neh! She’s too noisy, Mama! Tinu won’t stop crying.

blocks.text: Neh! Neh!
She’s too noisy, Mama! Tinu won’t stop crying.

pages.text: She’s trying to tell you something, Tayo. What do you think she’s saying? I don’t know, says Tayo.

blocks.text: She’s trying to tell you something, Tayo. What do you think she’s saying?
I don’t know, says Tayo.

pages.text: Well, I think she’s just hungry. Mama feeds Tinu. But…

blocks.text: Well, I think she’s just hungry. Mama feeds Tinu. But…

pages.text: Eh! Tinu cries again. You’ve fed her, why is she still crying? Because need sometimes, a good… babies

blocks.text: Eh! Tinu cries again.
You’ve fed her, why is she still crying?
Because need sometimes, a good… babies
BUUURP!

pages.text: Friends and family want to meet her, but Tinu has never been around so many people. WAAAAHH! Mama takes Tinu to her room for some quiet time.

blocks.text: Friends and family want to meet her, but Tinu has never been around so many people. WAAAAHH! Mama takes Tinu to her room for some quiet time.

pages.text: As time goes by, Tayo learns what Tinu’s cries mean. And she doesn’t seem so bad after all. They even play together.

blocks.text: As time goes by, Tayo learns what Tinu’s cries mean.
And she doesn’t seem so bad after all. They even play together.

pages.text: No, Tinu. Planes are for flying, not chewing. But sometimes, Tinu cries and Tayo doesn’t know what she wants.

blocks.text: No, Tinu. Planes are for flying, not chewing.
But sometimes, Tinu cries and Tayo doesn’t know what she wants.

pages.text: Tinu cries through the day... and through the night. Why won’t she stop crying?

blocks.text: Tinu cries through the day...
and through the night.
Why won’t she stop crying?

pages.text: Wait, what’s that? Look Mama, Tinu’s got her first tooth!

blocks.text: Wait, what’s that? Look Mama,
Tinu’s got her first tooth!
